mission.title: Install and Configure Kind (Kubernetes in Docker)
mission.description: Kind (Kubernetes in Docker) is a tool for running local Kubernetes clusters using Docker container nodes. It is primarily designed for testing Kubernetes itself but can also be used for local development or CI. Key trade-offs include the requirement for Docker and the fact that it is not suitable for production workloads.

steps.title: Install Kind
steps.description:
This step installs Kind using the precompiled binary for your operating system.

```bash

[ $(uname -m) = x86_64 ] && curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.32.0/kind-$(uname)-amd64

[ $(uname -m) = aarch64 ] && curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.32.0/kind-$(uname)-arm64

chmod +x ./kind

sudo mv ./kind /usr/local/bin/kind

```

> For AMD64 / x86_64 on Linux
> For ARM64 on Linux
> Make the binary executable
> Move Kind to a directory in your PATH

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a local Kubernetes cluster using Kind.

```bash

kind create cluster --image kindest/node:v1.31.0

```

> Creates a cluster with Kubernetes version 1.31.0

steps.title: Set Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step sets up the kubeconfig file to interact with the Kind cluster.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=$(kind get kubeconfig-path)

```

> Set the KUBECONFIG environment variable to the Kind cluster's kubeconfig

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step verifies that the nodes in the Kind cluster are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Check the status of the nodes in the cluster

uninstall.title: Remove Kind Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kind cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all workloads and data in the cluster.

```bash

kind delete cluster

```

> Delete the Kind cluster

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kind
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup any important data. This step upgrades Kind to the latest version.

```bash

curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.32.0/kind-$(uname)-amd64

chmod +x ./kind

sudo mv ./kind /usr/local/bin/kind

```

> Download the latest Kind binary
> Make the binary executable
> Replace the old Kind binary

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kind: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kind binary is not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Ensure that /usr/local/bin is in your PATH or move the Kind binary to a directory that is in your PATH.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: failed to start docker container'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Docker is not running or not installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Start Docker and ensure it is running properly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no nodes available in cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Run 'kind create cluster' again and check for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubelet is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubelet service may not have started correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Kind node using 'docker logs <node-name>' to diagnose the issue.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.31, improved error messages during cluster creation.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with the status 'Ready' when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure Docker is installed and running on your machine.
